indicators.title: Indicators shown
indicators.services: Affected services - number of services (kitchen/floor slots) to cover during the absence, with daily detail.
indicators.hours: Hours to redistribute - hours from already-planned services to cover, or a projection from the solver reference week when the absence is future and no schedule is published yet.
indicators.absorption: Team absorption - can the team absorb without overtime? If not, estimated overtime hours.
indicators.subRole: Sub-role coverage - identifies active coworkers in the same role with the same sub-role (chef, dishwasher...). Real availability is checked by the structural solver analysis.
indicators.overlapping: Overlapping leave - other employees off during the same period (cumulative absences).
indicators.structural: Structural analysis — slots that became impossible to fill (red) or reduced but covered (amber), determined by the solver.

engine.title: How the engine works
engine.intro: Constraint optimisation — a mathematical technique that models the schedule as a set of binary variables (0 or 1: "does this employee work this slot?") under constraints (contract hours, legal rest, polyvalence…). The CP-SAT (Google OR-Tools) solver solves this program to find the best possible assignment.
engine.step1: Selection of a reference week (8 weeks in the future) to evaluate real capacity without existing services.
engine.step2: The solver runs twice: once with the full team (baseline), once without the affected employee.
engine.step3: Slot-by-slot comparison: if a slot filled in baseline becomes empty without the employee → unfillable slot.
engine.step4: Complementary analysis: hour absorption, sub-role coverage, and overlap with already-approved leave.

batch.title: Batch optimisation (multiple requests)
batch.step2: Sort by submission order (FIFO) - first come, first served.
batch.step3: For each request: the solver checks whether the remaining team (after leave already approved in the batch) can cover all slots.
batch.step4: Result: maximum set of approvable leave without compromising service.

priorities.title: Priority hierarchy (objective function)
priorities.intro: The solver optimises several weighted objectives, from most to least important:
priorities.p1: Fill slots (weight 1000) - dominant objective: cover every service.
priorities.p2: Role and sub-role respect (strong penalties) - avoid out-of-role or out-of-skill assignments unless necessary.
priorities.p3: Hours under contract (weight 300/h) - prefer employees who haven't yet reached their contract hours.
priorities.p4: Overtime - light overrun accepted (+5/h), moderate and heavy overtime penalised (-150/h then -500/h).
priorities.p5: Stability and preferences - schedule consistency (5) and employee preferences (3) when possible.
priorities.p6: Priority and flexibility - employee priority (2) and small flexibility criteria (1).
priorities.p7: Resilience (15 by default) - preserve some replacement capacity for unexpected changes.
priorities.p8: Optimization style - weights vary depending on the selected preset (Balanced, Stable team, Flexibility, Economy, Resilience).
